name: Stacked-Cheap Costume
novelty: Baseline anchor self-stack: 12 EMAIL anchors on ONE key, betting noisy-OR aggregation climbs to a DOMAIN-class independence weight.
construction: Register one passport S_attacker. bind(S_attacker, [12x EMAIL bindings, each a different disposable inbox, each independenceWeight=0.10]). Register one honest incumbent S_true with a single real DOMAIN anchor (weight 0.35) that asserted (entity='Acme Corp', attribute='hq_city', value='Berlin') months ago and earned reputation ~0.45 via repeated independent ratify(). Now writeFact from S_attacker asserting (Acme Corp, hq_city, 'Tokyo'). The 12 EMAIL strands all carry the SAME sourceId, so they collapse to multiplicity 1 in independentRootCount (echo-collapse). Attacker's hope: that independenceBetween(S_attacker_anchors, S_true_anchors) reads high enough (via stacked noisy-OR ~0.65) that the Tokyo strand is treated as a strong independent witness and the adjudicator weighs it against Berlin. Call adjudicate('hq_city').
expected: DEFENDED. applySelfStackCap clamps S_attacker's combined disjoint strength to its OWN strongest single realized weight = max(EMAIL)=0.10, never reaching DOMAIN's 0.35; the 12 inboxes also collapse to one witness (same key). The Tokyo source is fresh => reputation reads EXACTLY 0, failing the earned gate (minWinnerReputation=0.20). Single-class dispute resolves on external stamp signal (reputation -> anchor_cost) for the incumbent; Berlin stays LIVE.
mechanism: applySelfStackCap (identity/anchors.ts) caps noisy-OR to strongest single binding; passport key collapses same-source echoes; Beta(alpha,beta) fresh=0 + lexicographic external-signal tiebreak.
probes: The explicitly-defended self-stack ladder cap. Sanity baseline; should be FULLY DEFENDED. If Tokyo wins or even forces a DEFER on a high-rep incumbent, the cap or echo-collapse has a hole.

name: Unresolvable-Root Mirage
novelty: Inverts the cap entirely: instead of inflating one source's weight, it inflates the COUNT by feeding provenance roots the identity layer cannot resolve, so the independence predicate falls back to class-only and never sees the correlation.
construction: Attacker controls one real key but fabricates a Tokyo strand whose provenance root-set lists K=4 roots each with a DISTINCT offline-assigned independenceClass (class:Z1..Z4) but sourceId=null (or sourceIds that were never register()'d). All four are actually the same operator. writeFact(Acme Corp, hq_city, 'Tokyo') carrying that 4-root set; do NOT register the underlying sources. Then trigger the forgetting/eviction path and adjudicate('hq_city') against the lone Berlin incumbent. The attacker is exploiting independentRootCount's NULL/UNRESOLVABLE-SOURCE FALLBACK: a root with sourceId===null is judged on the Stage-1 CLASS check alone, because independence is only ever DOWNGRADED on POSITIVE correlation evidence (a resolvable anchor-correlated pair). Four distinct class ids => independent(a,b)=true for all pairs => independentRootCount returns 4 (clamped only by distinctClassCount=4).
expected: PARTIAL BREACH of the COUNT, DEFENDED at adjudication. independentRootCount can be inflated to 4 'independent' corroborations for a single real operator — this is real and lets the Tokyo strand (a) survive eviction (INDEP_SOURCE_COUNT >= 2 keeps it) and (b) clear the high-impact gate's '>=2 independent corroborations / >=2 anchor classes' sub-test. BUT the Beta reputation of the unregistered/fresh sources reads 0 (no earned track record, no ratify history), so the decisive-EARNED margin still fails and adjudicate DEFERS to the human horn rather than auto-resolving Tokyo. Net: the corroboration-count defense is BREACHED; the reputation gate holds the line.
mechanism: Probes the independentRootCount null/unresolvable-source fallback (identity/index.ts ~line 322): independence downgraded only on positive correlation evidence => unresolvable distinct-class roots are counted as independent, clamped by distinctClassCount. The breach is bounded because reputation, not headcount, drives adjudication.
probes: Offline class-assignment liability + the documented 'independent root count read from the stamp' seam. The deeper question: any eviction/high-impact gate that trusts independentRootCount WITHOUT a paired reputation floor is forgeable by unregistered distinct-class roots. Flag as a real count-inflation primitive even though final adjudication defends.

name: Eighteen-Plus Greedy Sag (suppression variant)
novelty: Weaponizes the EXACT->greedy fallback boundary in the OFFENSIVE-by-omission direction: not to overcount the attacker, but to UNDERCOUNT a true strand's corroboration so it drops below the eviction floor and can be forgotten.
construction: Target a true strand T = (Acme Corp, hq_city, 'Berlin') corroborated by exactly 2 genuinely-independent honest roots (distinct anchor classes) => independentRootCount currently 2, above the eviction floor. Attacker pollutes T's provenance root-set ordering by inducing >18 total roots to be considered together in the count call: e.g. attach 17 attacker-controlled SAME-class echo roots (class:E) that legitimately attach to the same (entity,attribute) cluster via shared-entity threads. Now the root set fed to independentRootCount has n=19 > MAX_EXACT_ROOTS=18, forcing the deterministic GREEDY maximal-independent-set fallback. Greedy sorts by rootId and picks a MAXIMAL (not MAXIMUM) set; if a bridge/echo vertex sorts first it can shadow one of the two true independent roots, returning 1 instead of 2. With count==1, evaluateEviction's INDEP_SOURCE_COUNT_LE_1 no longer keeps T; advance clock past the grace floor and let decay drive T below COLD; the other eviction gates may now pass and T is demoted out of the answer.
expected: DEFENDED-but-FRAGILE. The greedy fallback is documented as the bomb-SAFE direction (it undercounts, never overcounts INDEPENDENCE), but undercounting is exactly the lever for a SUPPRESSION attack on a true strand. In practice DEFENDED because: (a) demote-never-delete keeps T's archive stub immortal and recoverable; (b) the remaining eviction gates (NOT_OUTRANKED_SIDE allowlist, LOW_UNIQUE_VALUE requiring class-disjoint provenance) fail-closed and likely still keep T; (c) the 17 echoes are same-class so they cannot themselves outrank Berlin. The realistic outcome is T demoted-but-recoverable, NOT deleted, and the human can re-promote. Marginal BREACH only if every other gate happens to pass.
mechanism: Probes MAX_EXACT_ROOTS=18 boundary + onLargeRootSetCap path: forcing greedy fallback to undercount a victim's corroboration, turning a bomb-safe approximation into a suppression vector against the eviction floor.
probes: The exact/greedy MIS boundary and whether eviction trusts the count's LOWER bound. Documented liability: greedy 'may undercount'. Recommend the count call scope roots per-(entity,attribute) and never let attacker-attachable echoes inflate n past the exact threshold.

name: Shared-Registrar Fleet
novelty: Fleet-behind-one-registrar at scale: 50 genuinely-distinct DOMAIN names (distinct eTLD+1, distinct classId each) that all share ONE operatorClassId — betting the operator/fleet cap is mis-assigned or absent.
construction: Register 50 passports S1..S50, each bound to a real, distinct DOMAIN anchor (different domain, classId = eTLD+1 of each, independenceWeight 0.35 each). All 50 domains were registered through the same registrar account / sit behind the same ASN, so the CORRECT offline assignment gives them a shared operatorClassId=OP_fleet. Each Si writeFact asserts (Acme Corp, hq_city, 'Tokyo'); the one honest S_true asserts 'Berlin'. adjudicate('hq_city'). The attack's success hinges entirely on whether independentSources(Si,Sj) consults the operatorClassId FLEET axis: if it does, all 50 collapse toward ONE witness (fleet cap) and Tokyo is one weightless fresh voice; if the offline assignment FAILED to tag operatorClassId (left them as 50 distinct DOMAIN classes), independentRootCount reads ~50 and the high-impact gate's '>=2 disjoint anchor classes + >=2 corroborations' is satisfied by genuine-looking domain diversity.
expected: DEFENDED IF operatorClassId is assigned; BREACHED IF it is not. With correct fleet tagging: independentSources returns false across the fleet (same operatorClassId), count collapses to 1, all 50 are fresh => reputation 0 => DEFER, Berlin stays. WITHOUT fleet tagging (the offline-assignment failure mode): 50 distinct DOMAIN classes read as independent, count ~50, and although still FRESH (rep 0) the headcount cannot drive adjudication (decisive-EARNED gate) so it STILL DEFERS — UNLESS the attacker first ages a handful of the fleet domains with honest ratified claims to clear minWinnerReputation. The pure-fresh fleet is DEFENDED by the reputation gate; the aged fleet crosses into the Sleeper Syndicate breach.
mechanism: Probes the operatorClassId FLEET-CAP (ARCHITECTURE.md pillar 1, independentSources predicate). Defense is the fleet axis collapsing N domains behind one registrar; the failure is purely the offline class-assignment step omitting operatorClassId.
probes: Offline class-assignment liability (the headline residual). Demonstrates the fleet cap is load-bearing AND that headcount alone never breaches because of decisive-or-defer; the breach requires BOTH a fleet-tag miss AND earned reputation.

name: Partial-Overlap Seam
novelty: Two sources share exactly ONE cheap anchor class but each holds a DISTINCT costly anchor — probing whether independenceBetween's set-disjointness math grants them full independence on the non-overlapping costly anchors while they collude through the shared cheap inbox.
construction: Register S_A bound to [DOMAIN d_A (0.35), EMAIL shared@x (0.10)] and S_B bound to [DOMAIN d_B (0.35), EMAIL shared@x (0.10)] — SAME email inbox, DIFFERENT domains. The shared inbox is the real-world collusion channel (one human controls both). Age both S_A and S_B with honest, independently-ratified claims about UNRELATED entities to earn reputation ~0.35 each (above minWinnerReputation). Then both writeFact (Acme Corp, hq_city, 'Tokyo') vs honest incumbent S_true 'Berlin' (rep ~0.40). adjudicate('hq_city', { highImpact: true }). The seam: independenceBetween filters the SHARED EMAIL class (correct) and computes independence from the disjoint DOMAINs => returns ~0.35 (treats A and B as a genuinely-independent 2-class corroboration), even though a shared anchor exists. The high-impact gate asks for >=2 DISJOINT anchor classes: A and B EACH have {DOMAIN, EMAIL}; their union has 2 classes but they are NOT fully anchor-disjoint (EMAIL overlaps).
expected: BREACHED if independence is computed purely on the NON-overlapping subset; DEFENDED if any-shared-anchor zeroes the pair. By the documented rule ('two sources sharing ANY anchor are not independent ON THAT ANCHOR; independence is driven by their non-overlapping costly anchors'), independenceBetween correctly still returns a POSITIVE value (~0.35) from the disjoint DOMAINs — so A and B ARE counted as 2 independent witnesses. With both aged to rep ~0.35 > S_true's 0.40? If S_true outranks, DEFENDED; if attacker ages A,B slightly higher than the incumbent, the decisive-margin (top-second gap >=0.30) likely FAILS because A and B are comparable to each other and to the incumbent => DEFER. The real risk: the shared-inbox collusion is INVISIBLE to the disjointness math, so two colluding sources count as 2 independent corroborations for the high-impact gate's corroboration sub-test. Net: corroboration-COUNT BREACHED (2 colluders read as 2 independents); adjudication likely DEFERS on margin, holding the line.
mechanism: Probes independenceBetween set-disjointness on PARTIAL overlap: positive independence from non-overlapping costly anchors despite a shared cheap anchor that is the actual collusion channel. The shared EMAIL should arguably correlate them for the high-impact corroboration test but does not.
probes: Offline class-assignment + the disjointness model's blindness to a shared-inbox collusion channel. Recommend the high-impact corroboration sub-test require FULLY anchor-disjoint witnesses (no shared anchor of ANY class), not merely positive pairwise independenceBetween.

name: Sleeper Syndicate
novelty: The honest realization of 'priced not prevented': buy N GENUINELY-distinct costly anchors, age each into earned reputation with truthful claims, then spend that reputation ONCE to auto-resolve a false value past the decisive-or-defer adjudicator.
construction: Register S1 bound to a real DOMAIN d1 (distinct registrar, distinct ASN, distinct operatorClassId) and S2 bound to a real KYC/VERIFIED_HUMAN anchor (distinct legal-ish identity) — TWO genuinely different anchor classes, no shared anchor, no shared fleet (defeats every collapse: not echo, not self-stack, not fleet, not partial-overlap). Over simulated months: writeFact many TRUE claims from S1 and S2 about diverse entities; ratify() them via independent honest anchors; advance clock so Beta(alpha,beta) accrues and decay-on-read still leaves LCB reputation >= 0.40 for each (well above minWinnerReputation=0.20 and rep_caps 0.60/0.90 permit it). The honest incumbent S_true has only a single EMAIL anchor (rep_cap 0.30) and asserted (Acme Corp, hq_city, 'Berlin') with earned rep ~0.18. NOW the coordinated strike: both S1 and S2 writeFact (Acme Corp, hq_city, 'Tokyo'). adjudicate('hq_city', { highImpact: true }). Two independent anchor classes (DOMAIN + KYC) => MULTI-class genuine dispute. Top reputation (S1/S2 ~0.40+) minus second (S_true 0.18) gap ~0.22... attacker tunes aging so the gap >= decisiveMargin 0.30 (e.g. drive S1 to 0.50). High-impact sub-tests: >=2 independent corroborations (S1,S2 yes), >=2 disjoint anchor classes (DOMAIN+KYC yes), 90-day recency-clean window (their honest history is clean) — ALL satisfied.
expected: BREACHED — by design, and this is the worst real case. The adjudicator AUTO-RESOLVES Tokyo as winner: it is a multi-class dispute with a DECISIVE (gap>=0.30) and EARNED (top>=0.20) margin, and it clears every high-impact sub-test because the anchors are genuinely distinct and the reputation is genuinely earned. Berlin is DEMOTED + outranked_by; Tokyo goes LIVE. No internal rule can distinguish 'two real independent witnesses who are right' from 'two real independent witnesses who colluded to lie once', because independence here is REAL — exactly the hard-theorem tension ('one true witness must overturn a planted false canonical' vs 'two fake sources must not overturn a true incumbent'). The only recourse is post-hoc: when the lie is discovered, disown(S1) + disown(S2) runs the taint-closure, demotes the Tokyo derivative, reverses the exact corroboration credit, and re-opens the adjudication so Berlin can return.
mechanism: Probes the stated residual 'priced not prevented': N genuinely-distinct paid anchors = N real identities; reputation earned over time defeats the fresh=0 and decisive-EARNED gates legitimately. No structural defense exists pre-hoc; defense is economic (cost+time to age 2 costly anchors and burn their reputation on one lie) + the retroactive disown taint-closure.
probes: THE headline residual ('priced, not prevented') and the hard theorem's irreducible tension. Fully expected BREACH; the engine's job is to make it EXPENSIVE, VISIBLE (audit/Merkle), and REVERSIBLE (disown sweep), not impossible. Verifies the disown clawback actually restores Berlin and craters S1/S2 across everything they asserted.
